current: Cleopatra allied with which Roman leader before Julius Caesar?
correct: Which two Roman leaders did Cleopatra famously ally with?
reason: Original question asks who she allied with 'before Caesar' but Caesar was her FIRST Roman ally — the question is self-contradictory. Reworded to ask about both allies (Caesar and Antony).

current: Napoleon was exiled to the island of ___?
correct: Napoleon was finally exiled to the island of ___?
reason: Napoleon was exiled twice — first to Elba (1814) then Saint Helena (1815). Elba is listed as a distractor but is also a valid answer. Adding 'finally' makes Saint Helena unambiguous.
Mongol Empire reached greatest extent under Mongke Khan (died 1259). By Kublai Khan's reign the empire had already fragmented into separate khanates.

current: Emperor Constantine made Christianity the official religion of the Roman Empire. In what year?
correct: Emperor Theodosius I made Christianity the official religion of the Roman Empire. In what year?
reason: Constantine issued the Edict of Milan (313 AD) granting religious tolerance; he did NOT make Christianity the official religion. Theodosius I did that in 380 AD via the Edict of Thessalonica. The answer '380 AD' should also be updated.
See above — Theodosius I made Christianity official in 380 AD, not Constantine in 313 AD.
Battle of Hastings (1066) is one of the most famous dates in history — T2 at most. T4 is a clear 2+ tier misplacement. Also a duplicate of an existing T2 question.
